Performance_Levels.Name: Exemplary
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Complete mastery of topic with in-depth analysis and originality. Addresses all key issues, applies relevant models, integrates evidence, and proposes well-justified solutions.

Performance_Levels.Name: Accomplished
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Solid understanding with some analysis/originality. Addresses most issues and models, but depth/integration uneven. Solutions reasonable but not fully developed.

Performance_Levels.Name: Developing
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Limited understanding and minimal analysis. Misses key issues, weak use of models/evidence, incomplete solutions.

Performance_Levels.Name: Beginning
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Little to no understanding; summary only, no analysis or models, no viable solutions.

Performance_Levels.Name: Exemplary
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Effectively integrates data, charts, graphs, or forecasting models. Evidence is clearly explained and supports conclusions. Strong analytical skills shown.

Performance_Levels.Name: Accomplished
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Includes relevant data/models, but explanation or integration is partial. Some evidence underdeveloped.

Performance_Levels.Name: Developing
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Uses limited or generic evidence. Models/data poorly explained or loosely connected.

Performance_Levels.Name: Beginning
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: No meaningful use of data, models, or evidence.

Performance_Levels.Name: Exemplary
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Clear organization with strong logic/flow. Well-structured sections with effective headings. APA 7th followed without errors.

Performance_Levels.Name: Accomplished
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Organization clear but minor lapses in flow/structure. Few APA errors.

Performance_Levels.Name: Developing
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Organization uneven/confusing. Frequent APA errors.

Performance_Levels.Name: Beginning
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Paper poorly organized, difficult to follow. APA not observed.

Performance_Levels.Name: Exemplary
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Provides thoughtful evaluation of findings, discusses implications, and offers well-supported recommendations. Questions assumptions and suggests improvements.

Performance_Levels.Name: Accomplished
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Some evaluation and recommendations, but limited depth. Recommendations may be generic.

Performance_Levels.Name: Developing
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Limited evaluation; more summary than analysis. Weak/vague recommendations.

Performance_Levels.Name: Beginning
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: No evaluation or recommendations.

Performance_Levels.Name: Exemplary
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: The written assignment is free of grammatical and spelling errors.

Performance_Levels.Name: Accomplished
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: The written assignment contains few grammatical and/or spelling errors.

Performance_Levels.Name: Developing
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: The written assignment contains multiple grammatical and/or spelling errors.

Performance_Levels.Name: Beginning
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: The written assignment has numerous grammar and/or spelling errors. The document is difficult to read.

Performance_Levels.Name: Exemplary
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Presentation demonstrates mastery of the case study with in-depth analysis and originality. Key issues are clearly identified, models/data are applied appropriately, and implications/recommendations are well supported.

Performance_Levels.Name: Accomplished
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Presentation covers most key issues with some analysis and originality. Some evidence/models applied, though depth is uneven. Recommendations are reasonable but may lack full support.

Performance_Levels.Name: Developing
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Presentation includes limited analysis. Key issues are touched on but underdeveloped. Weak or generic application of evidence/models. Recommendations vague.

Performance_Levels.Name: Beginning
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Presentation shows little understanding of the case. Content is superficial, inaccurate, or off topic. No meaningful recommendations.

Performance_Levels.Name: Exemplary
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Data, charts, or visuals are accurate, clearly explained, and effectively integrated into the presentation. Visuals enhance understanding and support conclusions.

Performance_Levels.Name: Accomplished
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Data/visuals are relevant, but explanation or integration is partial. Visuals may not fully support conclusions.

Performance_Levels.Name: Developing
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Minimal or generic use of data/visuals. Integration into the presentation is weak or unclear

Performance_Levels.Name: Beginning
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: No use of data/visuals, or visuals are irrelevant/confusing.

Performance_Levels.Name: Exemplary
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Presentation is well-organized, logical, and easy to follow. Strong introduction, transitions, and conclusion. Visual aids (slides, structure) enhance clarity.

Performance_Levels.Name: Accomplished
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Organization is clear overall, with minor lapses in flow or structure. Visual aids generally support clarity.

Performance_Levels.Name: Developing
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Minimal or generic use of data/visuals. Integration into the presentation is weak or unclear.

Performance_Levels.Name: Beginning
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Presentation is disorganized and difficult to follow. Visual aids absent or ineffective.

Performance_Levels.Name: Exemplary
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Font formatting (color bold, italic) has been carefully planned to enhance readability and content

Performance_Levels.Name: Accomplished
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Font formatting (color bold, italic) has been carefully planned to enhance readability.

Performance_Levels.Name: Developing
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Font formatting (color bold, italic) has been carefully planned to complement the content. However a little hard to read.

Performance_Levels.Name: Beginning
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Font formatting makes it very difficult to read the material.

Performance_Levels.Name: Exemplary
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Meets slide requirement and includes video or speaker notes (depending on assignment).

Performance_Levels.Name: Accomplished
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Close to target length; included video or speaker notes (depending on assignment).

Performance_Levels.Name: Developing
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: PowerPoint exceeded slide requirement; did not included video or speaker notes (depending on assignment).

Performance_Levels.Name: Beginning
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Did not meet slide requirement and did not include video or speaker notes (depending on assignment).

Performance_Levels.Name: Exemplary
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Presentation demonstrates mastery of the case study with in-depth analysis and originality. Key issues are clearly identified, models/data are applied appropriately, and implications/recommendations are well supported.

Performance_Levels.Name: Accomplished
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Presentation covers most key issues with some analysis and originality. Some evidence/models applied, though depth is uneven. Recommendations are reasonable but may lack full support.

Performance_Levels.Name: Developing
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Presentation includes limited analysis. Key issues are touched on but underdeveloped. Weak or generic application of evidence/models. Recommendations vague.

Performance_Levels.Name: Beginning
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Presentation shows little understanding of the case. Content is superficial, inaccurate, or off topic. No meaningful recommendations.

Performance_Levels.Name: Exemplary
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Presentation is well-organized, logical, and easy to follow. Strong introduction, transitions, and conclusion.

Performance_Levels.Name: Accomplished
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Organization is clear overall, with minor lapses in flow or structure.

Performance_Levels.Name: Developing
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Organization is uneven or confusing at times. Lacked clarity or consistency.

Performance_Levels.Name: Beginning
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Presentation is disorganized and difficult to follow.

Performance_Levels.Name: Exemplary
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Presenter communicates in a confident, professional, and engaging manner. Voice is clear, pace appropriate, and narration is strong. Very few filler words.

Performance_Levels.Name: Accomplished
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Presenter speaks clearly, with some confidence and engagement. Some filler words or pacing issues, but message is understandable.

Performance_Levels.Name: Developing
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Delivery is inconsistent, with frequent filler words, unclear articulation, or distracting mannerisms.

Performance_Levels.Name: Beginning
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Presenter is unprepared or disengaged. Delivery unclear, rushed, or inaudible.

Performance_Levels.Name: Exemplary
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Meets required time (e.g., 5–7 minutes) Content thorough but not rushed.

Performance_Levels.Name: Accomplished
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Close to target length; pacing is mostly appropriate.

Performance_Levels.Name: Developing
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Too short or too long; pacing uneven. Content lacks appropriate depth for time allotted.

Performance_Levels.Name: Beginning
Performance_Levels.Grading_Criteria: Did not meet required time (e.g., 5–7 minutes).
